This stage is cross-cutting infrastructure: it does not run as a startup sequence or main-loop subsystem itself, but supplies the shared contracts that let startup, runtime, persistence, plugins, tools, UI, and external clients agree on the same data shapes and semantics. At its center, the protocol crate defines the wire and persisted session model in protocol.rs, richer turn/item and user-input schemas in items.rs and user_input.rs, broad runtime/config-visible enums in models.rs, config_types.rs, openai_models.rs, approvals.rs, account.rs, auth.rs, capabilities.rs, dynamic_tools.rs, mcp.rs, memory_citation.rs, network_policy.rs, and parse_command.rs, plus core value objects such as SessionId, ThreadId, AgentPath, ToolName, shell output decoding, permissions, and the central error taxonomy.

Around that core, neighboring crates contribute equally reusable domain types: plugin IDs and manifests, normalized tool definitions and discovery records, loaded/effective config structs, cloud-task API traits, exec-policy decisions and validated invocations, network-proxy reason codes, skill catalogs and loaded-skill models, thread-store request/response schemas, state and graph lifecycle enums, unified-exec process snapshots, mention syntax constants, and TUI app/startup event types. Together these modules keep serialization, validation, compatibility, and type safety aligned across the whole system.
